figure.title: Counter and Data Mapping (Partial)
figure.purpose: Shows the mapping of the 3rd, 4th, and 5th 16-byte counters to specific bit ranges of the original data payload.

technical_summary: Displays the structure for AES-GCM counter blocks.
3rd Counter: Corresponds to Bytes [0-15] mapped to OrigData bits [255-127] (Note: typically [255-128] in 128-bit chunks).
4th Counter: Corresponds to Bytes [0-15] mapped to OrigData bits [383-256].
5th Counter: Corresponds to Bytes [0-15] mapped to OrigData bits [511-384].

critical_facts: Large payloads are broken into 128-bit (16-byte) chunks, each associated with a sequentially incremented counter block for AES-GCM encryption.
potential_ambiguities: The diagram shows [255-127] which is 129 bits. It is likely a typo for [255-128].
